skill.name: backtesting-frameworks
skill.description: Build robust backtesting systems for trading strategies with point-in-time data, walk-forward testing, transaction costs, and risk metrics.
skill.summary: Design and evaluate trading strategy backtests with bias controls, realistic costs, and robustness checks.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged code execution, shell execution, weak cryptography, credential files, reconnaissance, and network patterns. Review found these are Markdown diagrams, Python example code, finance terminology, inline file references, and book or documentation URLs, with no prompt injection or malicious behavior found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences and Diagrams Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static shell execution hits occur on Markdown code fences, ASCII diagrams, or inline resource paths. No shell command, Ruby backtick execution, or command injection vector is present in the reviewed context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown formatting and documentation references. There is no executable shell syntax outside fenced examples or inline path text.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Dynamic Function Constructor Hit Is Documentation Text
low_findings.description: The dynamic function constructor alert points to a docstring describing a strategy callback signature. It does not construct code from strings or execute user-controlled input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 403 is plain explanatory text inside a Python docstring. No eval, Function constructor, or dynamic code execution appears there.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Finance Terms Misread as Cryptographic or Credential Artifacts
low_findings.description: Weak cryptography and certificate or key alerts map to backtesting concepts, metric names, and parameter variable names. No cryptographic algorithm, certificate material, or private key content was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain table content, trade lists, metric fields, and parameter names. They do not include cryptographic primitives or sensitive file contents.

low_findings.title: Benign External Reference URLs
low_findings.description: The only confirmed network indicators are resource links to books and Backtrader documentation. They are passive Markdown links and do not perform network requests or transmit data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are static references in a Resources section. No code path fetches them or sends local data to them.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Alerts Are Backtesting Instructions
low_findings.description: Reconnaissance-related alerts correspond to strategy validation, generated signals, grid search, and point-in-time data guidance. No system or network discovery behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line context is financial backtesting methodology. No host enumeration, network scanning, or discovery commands are present.

low_findings.title: Critical Heuristic Dismissed After Context Review
low_findings.description: The heuristic combined code, network, and credential signals, but the underlying signals are documentation examples, passive links, and false matches. No malicious intent, exfiltration, or prompt injection was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: A semantic review of both scanned files found no active code execution path or credential access. The remaining risk is limited to normal external reference links.

content.value_statement: Trading backtests often overstate performance because of bias, missing costs, and weak validation. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code design realistic backtesting workflows and review results.
actual_capabilities: Defines backtest scope, universe, timeframes, and evaluation criteria.
Explains point-in-time data handling to reduce look-ahead and survivorship bias.
Provides event-driven and vectorized Python backtesting patterns.
Guides walk-forward optimization and out-of-sample validation.
Covers transaction costs, slippage, drawdown, and risk-adjusted performance metrics.
Includes Monte Carlo analysis patterns for strategy robustness checks.
limitations: Does not provide live trading execution or broker integration.
Does not provide investment advice or guarantee future returns.
Requires reliable historical data and careful data cleaning.
Example code must be adapted and tested before production use.

use_cases.title: Prototype a Strategy Backtest
use_cases.description: Create a structured Python backtest with signals, fills, costs, positions, and performance metrics.

use_cases.title: Review Backtest Validity
use_cases.description: Check a strategy for look-ahead bias, survivorship bias, overfitting, and missing transaction costs.

use_cases.title: Build Validation Workflows
use_cases.description: Design walk-forward tests, parameter searches, and Monte Carlo checks for strategy robustness.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Basic Backtest
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the backtesting-frameworks skill. Help me define a simple backtest plan for this strategy, including universe, data fields, timeframe, costs, and metrics: [describe strategy].

prompt_templates.title: Check for Biases
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the backtesting-frameworks skill. Review this backtest design for look-ahead bias, survivorship bias, selection bias, overfitting, and unrealistic execution assumptions: [paste design].

prompt_templates.title: Implement a Python Framework
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the backtesting-frameworks skill. Draft an event-driven Python backtesting structure for [asset class] with orders, fills, positions, slippage, commission, and equity curve reporting.

prompt_templates.title: Design Robustness Testing
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the backtesting-frameworks skill. Create a walk-forward and Monte Carlo validation plan for this strategy, including train windows, test windows, parameter grids, metrics, and failure criteria: [strategy details].

output_examples.input: A user asks for a backtest plan for a moving average momentum strategy.
output_examples.output: A structured plan covering hypothesis, universe, data requirements, signal timing, transaction costs, validation splits, and performance metrics.

output_examples.input: A user provides a strategy report with high Sharpe ratio and no costs.
output_examples.output: A review that flags missing slippage, overfitting risk, limited out-of-sample evidence, and needed robustness tests.

output_examples.input: A user wants to compare event-driven and vectorized backtest approaches.
output_examples.output: A practical comparison explaining speed, realism, execution modeling, data needs, and when each approach fits.

best_practices: Define the hypothesis, universe, timeframe, and acceptance criteria before testing.
Use point-in-time data and reserve out-of-sample periods for final evaluation.
Model commissions, slippage, market impact, and capacity before interpreting returns.
anti_patterns: Optimizing parameters across the full history and reporting the best result.
Ignoring delisted assets, corporate actions, or data revisions.
Presenting backtest performance as a prediction or investment recommendation.

faq.question: Does this skill execute trades?
faq.answer: No. It is for backtest design and analysis, not live trading or broker execution.

faq.question: Can it write Python backtesting code?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes patterns for event-driven and vectorized Python backtesting systems.

faq.question: Does it prevent look-ahead bias automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and review criteria, but the implementation must enforce correct data timing.

faq.question: Is this investment advice?
faq.answer: No. It helps evaluate methods and risks, but it does not recommend buying or selling assets.

faq.question: What data quality is required?
faq.answer: Use clean historical data with timestamps, corporate actions, delisted assets where relevant, and known adjustment rules.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: It is marked for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code compatibility.
